main_ingredients: white rice or thick rice noodles (bee hoon)

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of yong tau foo laksa with rice or rice noodles is approximately 450 g, comprising a selection of stuffed tofu and vegetable items in laksa broth with a portion of white rice or thick bee hoon.

nutrient_source.title: Yong Tau Foo (Laksa, with Rice/Noodles) — Singapore HPB Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Yong Tau Foo (Laksa, with Rice/Noodles), cr_id F08010068. All per-100g values are taken as reported.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — rice noodles and mixed soup-based dishes
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition, 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for yong tau foo laksa with rice/noodles as a composite dish. GI of 45 is an analogous estimate based on the GI of thick rice noodles (bee hoon) and white rice in a soup context, which typically range GI 40–50 in the University of Sydney GI Database. The coconut milk broth and protein/fat content of the dish further moderate the glycaemic response. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (45 × 45.1) / 100 ≈ 20.3, rounded to 20.4 for a 450 g serving providing approximately 45.1 g carbohydrate.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 384 mg per 100 g; a 450 g serving delivers approximately 1728 mg sodium, representing 75–86% of the recommended daily limit of 2000–2300 mg for hypertensive individuals.
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should limit portion size, request reduced-sodium laksa broth, avoid consuming the full broth, and monitor total daily sodium intake carefully when including this dish.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Carbohydrate 10.03 g per 100 g with dietary fibre only 0.75 g per 100 g; the refined white rice or thick bee hoon base has a GI of approximately 45 (low), but a 450 g serving delivers approximately 45 g carbohydrate with a glycaemic load of ~20, which is moderate.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with type 2 diabetes should reduce the rice or noodle portion, substitute brown rice or whole-grain noodles where available, and increase the proportion of vegetable and tofu items to lower the overall glycaemic load of the meal.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat approximately 3.5 g per 100 g from coconut milk laksa broth; a 450 g serving provides approximately 15.75 g saturated fat, exceeding the recommended daily limit of 14–20 g for cardiovascular risk management, alongside high sodium at 1728 mg per serving.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should limit broth consumption to reduce saturated fat and sodium intake, choose leaner yong tau foo items such as vegetables and plain tofu over fried or fish-paste-stuffed items, and avoid frequent consumption of this dish.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Saturated fat ~3.5 g per 100 g from coconut milk and potential nightshade vegetables (eggplant, chili) may contribute to a pro-inflammatory dietary profile in sensitive individuals.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should request substitution of nightshade vegetables (eggplant, chili), limit broth consumption to reduce saturated fat, and monitor symptom response when consuming coconut milk-based dishes.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Yong Tau Foo with Laksa Broth and Rice or Rice Noodles is a popular Singaporean hawker dish combining the customisable yong tau foo format with a rich, spiced coconut milk laksa broth, served with either steamed white rice or thick rice vermicelli (bee hoon). Yong tau foo items typically include fish-paste-stuffed tofu, bitter gourd, eggplant, okra, tofu puffs, and fish balls, which are simmered or blanched and then placed in the aromatic laksa broth made from coconut milk, lemongrass, galangal, shrimp paste (belacan), and dried shrimp. The dish has a creamy, mildly spicy, and savoury flavour profile with a soft, varied texture from the different stuffed items. Per 100 g, it provides 108 kcal, 4.05 g protein, 5.73 g fat (approximately 3.5 g saturated from coconut milk), 10.03 g carbohydrate, 1.87 g sugar, 0.75 g dietary fibre, and 384 mg sodium. The high sodium content (384 mg/100 g) is a primary clinical concern for hypertension and cardiovascular disease management. The moderate carbohydrate load with a GI of approximately 45 makes it suitable for type 2 diabetes patients only in controlled portions with substitution of refined carbohydrate bases. The dish is a staple at Singapore hawker centres and food courts, commonly consumed at lunch or dinner, and reflects the Peranakan and Chinese culinary heritage of Singapore.

ingredient_synonyms.tofu puff: tau pok, fried tofu puff, 豆腐泡

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F08010068), which is the primary approved source for Singaporean dishes. GI is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database data for rice noodles and white rice in soup context, documented as such. Saturated fat estimated at ~3.5 g/100 g based on known coconut milk composition in laksa broth; this single field is estimated rather than directly reported.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g, potassium_mg set to -1: detailed micronutrient, fatty acid fractionation, and moisture data for this composite Singaporean hawker dish are not individually reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry F08010068 as of 2026. saturated_fat_g value of 3.5 g/100 g is estimated from the known saturated fat contribution of coconut milk in laksa broth preparations; this is not directly reported in the HPB source and is flagged as an estimate. GI of 45 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database data for thick rice noodles and white rice in soup-based dishes; no direct GI measurement for this composite dish was found in approved sources. calcium_mg value of 1.0 mg/100 g and iron_mg value of 0.02 mg/100 g are as reported in the HPB source. The dietary_tags field in the input included 'vegetarian', which is inconsistent with the dish containing fish paste, fish balls, and shrimp paste; this tag has been removed in the output classification. is_halal set to false as shrimp paste (belacan) and fish paste sourcing cannot be confirmed as halal-certified without vendor verification.
